section_title: Governance: Designated Director and Principal Officer

content_blocks.text:
## 5. Governance: Designated Director and Principal Officer

The Company's AML/CFT/CPF governance structure is established in accordance with Chapter 3 of the 2026 FIU-IND Guidelines and Rule 7 of the PML Rules. The structure rests on a clear separation between the Designated Director (responsible for overall compliance and strategic oversight) and the Principal Officer (responsible for operational implementation, monitoring, and reporting). The Designated Director and the Principal Officer shall be separate individuals.

Given the Company's status as a listed entity, the AML/CFT/CPF governance structure additionally integrates with the Company's broader listed-entity governance framework, including reporting obligations to the Audit Committee and disclosures under applicable SEBI Listing Regulations and Companies Act requirements where relevant.

### 5.2 Principal Officer

#### 5.2.1 Appointment

In accordance with Rule 2(1)(f) of the PML Rules and Paragraph 3.2 of the 2026 Guidelines, the Company has designated a Principal Officer ("PO") at the management level, preferably not below the level of Head of Audit / Compliance / Risk. The PO is responsible for ensuring implementation of and compliance with the obligations imposed under Chapter IV of the PMLA and the PML Rules.

The name, designation, address, and contact details of the Principal Officer shall be communicated promptly to FIU-IND in accordance with Rule 7(1) of the PML Rules. Any change shall be updated through the FINGate portal within seven (7) days.

Given the Company's transaction volume profile, the Principal Officer shall be appointed at a level commensurate with the scale and complexity of the Company's operations, typically at the level of Chief Compliance Officer, Chief Risk Officer, or equivalent C-suite or senior management designation.

### 5.4 Internal Audit and Independent Annual Audit

The scope, cadence, and governance of internal audit and independent annual audit of AML/CFT/CPF controls are set out in Section 8.3 of this Policy.
